title: Strict Transport Security (HSTS) Missing
description: Ensure the server enforces HTTPS connections to prevent downgrade attacks.
fix: Add 'Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains' to server configuration.

title: Content Security Policy (CSP) Not Configured
description: A CSP prevents XSS and data injection attacks by restricting resources.
fix: Implement a restrictive CSP header: 'Content-Security-Policy: default-src \'self\'';

title: X-Frame-Options Header Missing
description: Protects against clickjacking attacks by preventing iframe embedding.
fix: Set 'X-Frame-Options: DENY' or 'SAMEORIGIN' in the web server response headers.

title: SQL Injection Detection
description: User input is directly concatenated into SQL queries, leading to data breaches.
fix: Use prepared statements, parameterized queries, or ORM frameworks consistently across the codebase.

title: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
description: Unsanitized input is reflected or stored in the web page, allowing JS execution.
fix: Implement strict input validation and context-aware output encoding (e.g. HTML entity encoding).

title: Open Directory Listing Enabled
description: Web server is configured to list directory contents, leaking sensitive files.
fix: Disable directory browsing (e.g., 'Options -Indexes' in Apache or 'autoindex off' in Nginx).

title: Server Version Disclosure
description: The server leaks its version information, aiding targeted exploitation.
fix: Set 'ServerTokens Prod' and 'ServerSignature Off' in Apache, or 'server_tokens off;' in Nginx.

title: Default Credentials Detected
description: System is using manufacturer default credentials.
fix: Immediately change default administrative credentials to strong, randomly generated passwords.

title: Exposed Git Repository
description: The .git directory is publicly accessible, leaking source code.
fix: Deny access to hidden directories (.git) in the web server configuration.

title: Outdated PHP Version
description: An unsupported version of PHP is in use, susceptible to known CVEs.
fix: Upgrade to a supported PHP version (e.g., PHP 8.2+) and migrate deprecated code.

title: Exposed Environment File (.env)
description: Environment variables file containing database passwords and API keys is public.
fix: Block access to .env files and move them outside the web root.

title: CORS Misconfiguration
description: CORS policy allows arbitrary origins to read sensitive data.
fix: Configure 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' to specific trusted domains. Never use '*' with credentials.

title: Open Redirect Vulnerability
description: Application redirects users to arbitrary external URLs based on input.
fix: Validate redirect targets against a strict allowlist of authorized domains.

title: Weak SSL/TLS Protocols
description: Server supports outdated encryption protocols vulnerable to interception.
fix: Disable TLS 1.0 and 1.1. Enforce TLS 1.2 or 1.3 with strong AEAD ciphers.

title: Missing HTTPOnly on Cookies
description: Session cookies lack the HTTPOnly flag, exposing them to XSS attacks.
fix: Set the 'HttpOnly' flag for all authentication and session-related cookies.

title: Missing Secure Flag on Cookies
description: Session cookies can be transmitted over unencrypted HTTP connections.
fix: Set the 'Secure' flag for all cookies to enforce transmission over HTTPS.

title: Sensitive Information in URL
description: Tokens or passwords are passed in the URL, risking exposure in logs and referrers.
fix: Use HTTP POST requests or Authorization headers for transmitting sensitive data.

title: XML External Entity (XXE) Injection
description: XML parser is configured to resolve external entities, allowing file reads.
fix: Disable external entity resolution (DTD) in the XML parsing library.

title: Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR)
description: Users can access other users' data by modifying IDs in parameters.
fix: Implement strict access control checks on every data request to verify ownership.

title: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
description: Server fetches arbitrary URLs provided by the user, accessing internal networks.
fix: Use a strict allowlist for outbound requests and disable unused URI schemes.

title: Unrestricted File Upload
description: Application allows upload of executable files like PHP or JSP.
fix: Validate file extensions, check magic bytes, and store uploaded files outside the web root.

title: Exposed Admin Panel
description: Administrative interfaces are publicly accessible.
fix: Restrict admin panel access using IP allowlisting and require MFA.

title: Vulnerable WordPress Plugin
description: An installed WordPress plugin has known vulnerabilities.
fix: Update the plugin to the latest version or replace it with a secure alternative.

title: HTTP Methods Options Enabled
description: Dangerous HTTP methods (PUT, DELETE, TRACE) are enabled on the server.
fix: Disable TRACE/TRACK methods and restrict PUT/DELETE to authorized endpoints.

title: Lack of Rate Limiting
description: Authentication endpoints lack rate limiting, allowing brute-force attacks.
fix: Implement rate limiting and account lockout mechanisms for login attempts.

title: Exposed Database Port
description: Database services are accessible from the public internet.
fix: Bind databases to localhost (127.0.0.1) or restrict access via firewall rules.

title: Missing CSRF Token
description: State-changing operations do not require a CSRF token.
fix: Implement anti-CSRF tokens (Synchronizer Token Pattern) on all forms.

title: Robots.txt Information Disclosure
description: The robots.txt file reveals sensitive administrative paths.
fix: Remove sensitive paths from robots.txt and rely on proper authentication instead.

title: Outdated jQuery Version
description: An old version of jQuery with known XSS vulnerabilities is used.
fix: Update jQuery to the latest stable release (e.g., 3.x).

title: Debug Mode Enabled
description: Application framework is running in debug mode, exposing stack traces.
fix: Set environment to 'production' and disable debug output in configuration.

title: Unauthenticated API Endpoint
description: API endpoints handling sensitive data do not enforce authentication.
fix: Apply authentication middleware (e.g., JWT, OAuth) to all private API routes.

title: Exposed SSH Port
description: SSH service is publicly accessible without restrictions.
fix: Restrict SSH access via IP allowlist, disable root login, and use key-based authentication.

title: Information Leakage via Error Messages
description: Verbose error messages reveal database schema or internal logic.
fix: Implement generic error messages for end-users and log detailed errors internally.

title: Insecure JWT Implementation
description: JWT accepts 'none' algorithm or uses a weak secret key.
fix: Enforce strong signing algorithms (RS256) and use a high-entropy secret key.

title: Exposed Backup Files
description: Backup archives or database dumps are publicly accessible.
fix: Move backup files outside the web root and restrict access to the backups directory.

title: Insecure Cross-Domain Policy
description: Flash or Silverlight policy files allow broad cross-domain access.
fix: Restrict policy files to trusted domains or remove them if no longer needed.

title: Missing Security TXT
description: The site lacks a security.txt file for vulnerability reporting.
fix: Publish a security.txt file under the /.well-known/ path with contact details.

title: Subdomain Takeover
description: A DNS record points to an unclaimed third-party service.
fix: Remove the dangling CNAME record or claim the resource on the third-party provider.

title: Cleartext Authentication
description: Credentials are transmitted over unencrypted HTTP.
fix: Enforce HTTPS for all authentication pages and redirect HTTP traffic to HTTPS.

title: Exposed Docker API
description: Docker daemon API is accessible, leading to full host compromise.
fix: Bind the Docker API to localhost or use mutual TLS (mTLS) for authentication.
